steps.title: Expand to standard form
steps.content:
Expand $x(kx - 40) = -8$ to get:
$$kx^2 - 40x + 8 = 0$$
This identifies the coefficients: $a = k$, $b = -40$, $c = 8$.

steps.title: Apply discriminant condition for two distinct solutions
steps.content:
For two distinct real solutions, the discriminant must be positive: $b^2 - 4ac > 0$.
Substitute:
$$(-40)^2 - 4(k)(8) > 0$$
$$1600 - 32k > 0$$
$$k < 50$$

steps.title: Find the greatest integer value
steps.content: Since $k$ is an integer constant and must satisfy $k < 50$, the greatest possible value is $k = 49$.
